name: barbell decline pullover
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips and your feet secured.
Hold the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the barbell in an arc motion behind your head, feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then return the barbell to the starting position by reversing the motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips and your feet secured. Hold the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Extend your arms above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows. Lower the barbell in an arc motion behind your head, feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders. Pause for a moment, then return the barbell to the starting position by reversing the motion. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
